ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for penne all'arrabbiata; closest parent 'pasta food product' (FOODON_00002473) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Pasta, cooked) is the closest defensible parent; no specific arrabbiata sauce pasta code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229953004 'Pasta (substance)' used as closest parent; no specific penne arrabbiata concept exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of penne all'arrabbiata is approximately 300 g, comprising cooked penne pasta with spicy tomato and chilli sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Pasta, cooked, enriched, without added salt — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 20121); supplemented with USDA FoodData Central entry for tomato-based pasta sauce (NDB 06932) for sauce component estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Penne all'arrabbiata is not listed as a single composite entry in USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked enriched pasta (NDB 20121) as the dominant component (~65% by weight) and USDA tomato-based pasta sauce (NDB 06932) as the sauce component (~35% by weight). The dish is vegan with no dairy or meat additions. Weighted composite per-100g values calculated accordingly. Sodium reflects sauce contribution. This is an estimated composite; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for pasta (penne, durum wheat, boiled)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI values for cooked durum wheat pasta in the range 35–50 (mean ~45). Penne all'arrabbiata uses durum wheat penne as its primary carbohydrate source. GI of 45 is used as the closest defensible analogous estimate for cooked durum wheat penne. The tomato-chilli sauce contributes negligible additional glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 59.4) / 100 = 26.7, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 19.8 × 3 = 59.4 g.

hypertension.guidance: A 300 g serving provides approximately 555 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should monitor portion size and request reduced-salt sauce preparation, as cumulative sodium from restaurant servings can approach 25% of the 2,300 mg daily limit in a single dish.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from durum wheat pasta carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 45 and a glycemic load of ~27 per standard 300 g serving, penne all'arrabbiata produces a moderate postprandial glucose response; individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit portion size to 150–200 g cooked pasta and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to blunt the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and absence of cholesterol in this vegan preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Penne all'arrabbiata in its vegan form contains minimal saturated fat (~0.6 g per 300 g serving) and no cholesterol, making it a heart-friendly pasta option; however, sodium from the tomato sauce should be moderated, and olive oil quantity controlled to keep total fat within cardiovascular dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Penne all'Arrabbiata is a classic Italian pasta dish originating from the Lazio region of central Italy, particularly associated with Roman cuisine. The name 'arrabbiata' means 'angry' in Italian, referring to the fiery heat of the red chilli peppers used in the sauce. The dish consists of penne rigate (ridged tubular pasta made from durum wheat semolina) cooked al dente and tossed in a sauce of ripe tomatoes, garlic, dried or fresh red chilli, olive oil, and fresh flat-leaf parsley. It is inherently vegan and vegetarian, containing no meat, dairy, or eggs. The texture combines the firm bite of well-cooked durum wheat pasta with the smooth, slightly chunky tomato sauce that clings to the ridges of the penne. The flavour profile is bold, tangy, garlicky, and spicy, with the heat level adjustable by varying the quantity of chilli. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy, carbohydrate-dominant meal providing approximately 108 kcal per 100 g, with low fat and moderate protein from the pasta. The durum wheat pasta has a low glycemic index (~45) due to its dense protein-starch matrix, making it a relatively slower-digesting carbohydrate source compared to refined white rice or bread. Sodium content is moderate and derives primarily from the tomato sauce. The dish is a good source of complex carbohydrates, dietary fibre, and provides small amounts of vitamin C, iron, and B vitamins from both the pasta and tomato components. In a clinical context, the low GI and absence of saturated fat make it suitable for cardiovascular health when portion-controlled, though the glycemic load per full restaurant serving (~27) warrants attention for individuals managing type 2 diabetes. The sodium content from restaurant preparations may be a concern for hypertensive individuals.
common_user_queries: How many calories in penne arrabbiata?
What is arrabbiata sauce made of?

confidence_reason: Penne all'arrabbiata is not available as a single composite entry in any primary regional approved source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked enriched pasta and tomato-based pasta sauce, weighted by estimated component proportions. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cooked durum wheat pasta. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are a weighted composite estimate: approximately 65% cooked durum wheat penne (USDA SR Legacy NDB 20121) and 35% tomato-based pasta sauce (USDA NDB 06932). No single approved-source composite entry for penne all'arrabbiata was identified. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based dish with no hydrogenated fats; confirmed zero per USDA source data for these components. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a fully vegan dish containing no animal products. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a vegan dish with no animal-derived ingredients; no B12 is present by nature. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero in this plant-based dish; no vitamin D-containing ingredients are present. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.8), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.3) are estimated from olive oil contribution in the sauce component. 7. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate for cooked durum wheat penne from the University of Sydney GI Database; GL per 300 g serving = 26.7 g. 8. The input record listed cuisine_tags including 'Pizza' and 'Chicken Dish' which are not applicable to this dish and have been disregarded. 9. This dish is served at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk, Singapore, at SGD 26.16. 10. Italian cuisine does not require mandatory multilingual names under Project Veritas v2 rules (mandatory multilingual rules apply to Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese cuisines only); local_names fields are therefore null for non-romanized fields. 11. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 (pasta food product) used as closest_parent; FoodEx2 A00LC (Pasta, cooked) used as closest_parent; SNOMED CT 229953004 (Pasta, substance) used as closest_parent. No exact ontology codes exist for penne all'arrabbiata as a specific dish.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full composite nutrient profile from USDA SR Legacy pasta and tomato sauce components. Calculated weighted per-100g values.

Iteration 2: Verified GI category threshold (GI=45 ≤ 55 → 'low'). Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases. Confirmed all zero values (trans_fat, cholesterol, vitamin_b12, vitamin_d) are documented with 'naturally zero' or 'confirmed zero' justification in notes. Confirmed all match_type values use only allowed strings. Confirmed no mandatory multilingual names required for Italian cuisine. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass → PASS.
